name: Feast Valley
short_description: Feast Valley is a strategy game where you build farms, factories, resource buildings, and houses. Produce and sell food while feeding your villagers. Manage your resources to grow your valley and create a successful food-producing valley.
detailed_description:
Feast Valley

Feast Valley is a strategy game focused on managing food and resources and feeding villagers to keep your village alive. At the end of each month, you must provide food, beds, and basic resources through the Feast menu. If you fail to meet the needs of your villagers, Lives will be deducted, which may result in losing the game. However, if you succeed, you will earn a certain amount of gold for each villager.

How do you produce food and other resources?

  • Food production: Food is produced through specialized buildings that help meet the needs of your villagers. Each building contributes a different amount of food, making it an essential part of your village's sustainability.

    • Some simple foods can be produced directly from individual buildings, but more complex foods require multiple buildings placed next to each other. For example, to produce bread in a Bakery, you need to place an Eggery, Mill, and Dairy next to it for it to work and produce bread.

  • Resource production: To obtain materials like wood and stone, you can build specialized buildings to produce these resources over time or purchase them from the Market when needed.

How do you build buildings?

When building, you will see random cards at the bottom of the screen, each representing a different building. You must place buildings next to each other, as no building can be placed alone. Some buildings require specific adjacent buildings to function efficiently, like a Bakery which requires a Mill, Dairy, and Eggery to produce bread. Smart placement of buildings will help you maximize production and grow your village more effectively.

Left Menus

  • Valley
    This menu gives you an overview of your village, including the current number of villagers, the expected growth for the next month, the amount of gold earned, energy levels, available wood, stone, and the number of beds required for your villagers to continue. It also shows information on food and other resource production.

  • Feast
    At the end of each month, you must choose the type and amount of food you want to provide for the villagers, then click the Feast button to move to the next month. If you don't have enough food or resources, Lives will be deducted.

  • Requests
    Through this menu, you can sell food for gold, but you cannot sell items individually. Offers come in the form of orders that include multiple food types in varying quantities and prices. Some orders offer doubled prices, so choose wisely to make the best deal.

  • Market
    The Market is where you can purchase the necessary buildings to expand your valley. Purchases require gold, wood, and stone, which you can obtain from the Market to boost your production and further develop your village.

Do you have what it takes to turn Feast Valley into a prosperous haven for all its inhabitants? 

I think you have the courage to be brave and take the adventure
